title: Altered mental status in a 24-year-old with diabetes
presentation: A 24-year-old female with type 1 diabetes is brought in by her roommate for confusion and deep rapid breathing over the past several hours. She ran out of insulin 2 days ago. Vitals: BP 104/68, HR 118, RR 28 and deep, Temp 37.2C, SpO2 98%. Exam: fruity breath odor, dry mucous membranes, diffuse abdominal tenderness, GCS 13.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Glucose >250 + anion gap acidosis + ketonemia = DKA. Start IV fluids first, then insulin drip. Monitor potassium before starting insulin.
